implemented_core_mechanics: 30 chapter campaign atlas across 6 frontier regions
square grid battlefield with drag camera panning and tap unit control
village fort keep and capital ownership economy
recruitment from captured keeps forts and capitals
marshal doctrine bonuses wired from the campaign layer into battle rules
commander order support action with area buff and sustain
multiple objective types through commander defeat capital capture survive turns and escort heir chapters
easy normal and hard enemy AI behavior with capture pressure and reinforcement timing
menu atlas briefing help pause and result overlays
tracked UI pack assignment gameplay art assignment audio bundle and icon export
animation_and_facing_evidence: GameView lerps unit render positions between tiles instead of snapping every action
unit tokens update faceX and faceY toward travel or target direction before movement and attacks
attack arcs hit flashes capture pulses and commander-order rings provide stateful combat feedback
GameView loads shared terrain and frame texture assets through AssetManager and BitmapFactory from game_art paths
known_limitations: The first playable scope uses generated regional map patterns rather than fully bespoke hand-authored layouts for all 30 chapters.
Primary units use animated heraldic war tokens instead of imported humanoid sprite animations because the current shared library lacks a tactics-ready medieval directional unit pack.
remaining_quality_notes: The implementation preserves the confirmed turn-based village-capture tactics identity instead of collapsing into tower defense or a tiny skirmish template.
A future art pass can replace the runtime war tokens with a dedicated medieval tactics sprite pack without changing the current battle systems or HUD structure.
